unit: Ὅ6 Unit 6: Systems of Linear Equations and Inequalities
topic: Systems of Linear Inequalities by Graphing

questions.question: What is the purpose of graphing multiple inequalities on the same coordinate plane in the context of the Rosetta Stone's translation?

options.A: To identify the language of the stone
options.C: To find the feasible region for building a new temple
options.D: To analyze the economic systems of ancient civilizations
options.E: To understand the astronomical observations of the Egyptians

questions.question: The Great Pyramid of Giza's original height $h$ and its current height $c$ are related by the inequality $h - c > $ $20$.
The Hanging Gardens of Babylon had a system of canals with a minimum depth of $5$ meters and a maximum depth of $15$ meters. What is the range of possible depths $d$?
In the ancient city of Pompeii, a bakery had to produce at least $50$ loaves of bread per day, and no more than $100$ loaves. What is the range of possible number of loaves $l$?
A historian is analyzing the population growth of ancient Athens. The population $p$ is bounded by the inequalities $p > 5000$ and $p < 20000$.
The construction of the Colosseum in ancient Rome required at least $100$ workers and no more than $500$ workers. What is the range of possible number of workers $w$?
In the ancient city of Angkor Wat, the temple's height $h$ and width $w$ were related by the inequality $h < 2w$.
A museum curator is analyzing the number of artifacts $a$ and the number of visitors $v$ in an ancient exhibition. The curator finds that $a > 50$ and $v < 200$.

Graph the system of inequalities $x + y > 2$ and $x - y < 1$ on the same coordinate plane. Identify the overlapping solution region and interpret the feasible region in the context of the ancient Silk Road.

The ancient city of Machu Picchu had a system of agricultural terraces with a minimum area of $100$ square meters and a maximum area of $500$ square meters. Write a system of inequalities to represent the possible areas $a$ of the terraces and graph the system on a coordinate plane. Identify the overlapping solution region and interpret the feasible region.

teacher_rules: Encourage students to use graph paper for the free response questions
Remind students to label their axes and provide a title for their graphs
Suggest that students use different colors or symbols to distinguish between the different inequalities
